member.name: Adrian Ramsay
member.synopsis: Adrian Ramsay is the Green Party MP for Waveney Valley , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the roles of Green Spokesperson (Health), Green Spokesperson (Dentistry), and Green Spokesperson (Environment, Food and Rural Affairs).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Pigs: Animal Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her Department is collecting data on the number of flexible crates being installed by pig farmers in England; and what assessment she has made of the potential impact of that v
writtenQuestions.answerText: No such data is being collected on the number of flexible crates being installed. As stated in the Animal Welfare Strategy, the Government wants to work with industry so that it ends the use of pig farrowing crates. A consultation on the transition to alt...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Animal Welfare: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, if her Department will undertake analysis of modifications required to farmed animal housing and transport in order to safeguard animal welfare in extreme heat.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the Animal Welfare Strategy, Defra has commissioned the Animal Welfare Committee to assess the likely impacts of climate change on farmed animals and what measures could be put in place by the farming sector to protect animals against these ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Livestock: Weather

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government does not hold data on the number of farmed animals that have required veterinary attention and/or died on-farm due to heat stress.Data from 2022 indicates that, in July and August 2022 when there was unprecedented hot weather in the UK, app...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Biofuels: Sustainable Development
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether his Department has assessed the potential impact of the United States withdrawing from the Paris Agreement and the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change on biomass sustainability
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government only supports the use of sustainable biomass in line with UK’s sustainability criteria, irrespective of country of origin. We have consulted on a Common Biomass Sustainability Framework in order to improve consistency across sectors and str...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Biofuels: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of reliance on imports from countries that have withdrawn from international climate agreements on the UK's biomass strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government only supports the use of sustainable biomass in line with UK’s sustainability criteria, irrespective of country of origin. We have consulted on a Common Biomass Sustainability Framework in order to improve consistency across sectors and str...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Biofuels: Sustainable Development
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether the proposed Common Biomass Sustainability Framework will require imported forest biomass to originate from countries that are Parties to the Paris Agreement.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has consulted on proposals to develop the Common Biomass Sustainability Framework and is analysing responses received. The final design of the framework, will be informed by the evidence and views gathered through the consultation. Common ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Biofuels: Sustainable Development
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether he plans to align future biomass sustainability criteria with the land-use, land-use change and forestry requirements set out in the EU Renewable Energy Directive III.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has consulted on proposals to develop a Common Biomass Sustainability Framework and is analysing responses received. As stated in the consultation (p18), the sustainability criteria used by other countries and regions will be one of our con...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Animal Experiments: Primates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what progress has been made to end the use of non-human primates in scientific testing.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to reducing the use of animals in science, including non‑human primates, through the Replacing Animals in Science strategy, which supports the development, validation and uptake of alternative methods. The use of non‑human prim...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether he has received representations from the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs on ensuring that the statutory duties of local highway authorities for
writtenQuestions.answerText: Officials in the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government have worked closely with those in the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs and the Department for Transport during the development of the Local Outcomes Framework to ensu...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Countryside: Access
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, pursuant to the answer of 27 October 2025 to Question 83666, how new permanent legal rights of public access will be created through the Government's proposals for nine new river walks
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra’s nine new river walks and three new national forests are being delivered as part of the wider commitment to ensure access to nature for everyone, as set out in the Environmental Improvement plan. Defra’s river walks programme has the potential to d...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Marine Protected Areas: Bottom Trawling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps the Department is taking towards a ban on bottom trawling in inshore waters.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Our comprehensive network of Marine Protected Areas (MPAs), now covering 40% of English waters, and over 50% of inshore waters, restrict bottom trawling in sensitive areas. Not all MPAs require restrictions on bottom trawling for example some mobile speci...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Food
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, when the action plan for the Good Food Cycle will be published.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Good Food Cycle, published in July 2025, set out the Government’s long-term vision for the UK food system, defining shared outcomes for a more resilient, affordable, healthy and sustainable future. Delivery against these outcomes is already underway a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Work and Pensions: Women against State Pension Inequality
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, how many meetings Ministers and officials in his Department have held with representatives of the Women Against State Pension Inequality (WASPI) campaign since September 2024; and whether his Department
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have made our position on this matter clear as set out to Parliament on the 29 January 2026. Women Against State Pension Inequality Ltd (WASPI) are seeking permission from the High Court to bring a Judicial Review on our response to the Ombudsman’s rep...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Horticulture: Peat
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, if she will set out the timetable for the publication of information on the total annual use of peat in the UK horticultural market during the period 1 January to 31 December 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Our contractors are currently collecting and collating the usage of peat and peat free alternative growing media for 2024 and 2025. We will publish this data in due course.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Horticulture: Peat
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, if she will publish a list of external stakeholders who have been consulted on the development of legislation to end the sales and supply of horticultural peat products.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra has engaged extensively with a broad range of stakeholders in the development of policy to end the sale of peat and peat containing products. This has included representatives from the horticulture sector, environmental organisations, retailers, gro...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: National Security: Environment Protection

writtenQuestions.answerText: On 20 January 2026, the Government published the Nature security assessment, which examined the potential impacts that might arise from global biodiversity loss and ecosystem degradation. It is a strategic assessment, developed across HM Government. It wa...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Areas: Reviews
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of publishing her Department's report, tatus of Defra’s critical systems to 2030 and beyond.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra has considered the potential merits of publishing material relating to the “Status of Defra’s critical systems to 2030 and beyond”. This work was an early-stage internal foresight exercise to support official-level discussion on long-term trends acr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Pigs: Animal Welfare

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Animal Welfare Act 2006 prohibits procedures which involve interference with the sensitive tissues or bone structure of an animal. The Mutilations (Permitted Procedures) (England) Regulations 2007 list exemptions, including pig tail docking, to which ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

registeredInterests.category: 6. Land and property portfolio with a value over £100,000 and where indicated, the portfolio provides a rental income of over £10,000 a year

items.text: Type of land/property: Residential property (House) Number of properties: 1 Location: Norfolk Interest held: until 17 December 2025 Ownership details: Co-owned with a family member Rental income: until 17 December 2025 Rental income details: Half the rental income is paid to the co-owner (Registered 22 August 2024; updated 22 December 2025)

The Green Party is in receipt of donations to support legal costs in one legal action in which I am named as a party. The legal proceedings do not concern my personal rights or liabilities, I do not personally receive any donations and the Green Party has agreed to fully indemnify me for any charges and expenses. Date interest arose: 25 October 2022 (Registered 2 August 2024)
Co-Leader of the Green Party (England and Wales). This is a non-executive part-time role. Date interest ended: 2 September 2025 (Registered 3 August 2024; updated 22 December 2025)
